summary: Bug Fix VERIFIED: Admin-created users can now verify passwords for transfer authorization. All 15 backend tests passed. The fix correctly handles STRING IDs (used by admin-created users) by trying ObjectId lookup first, then falling back to string lookup if result is None. Regular user flows and admin functionality continue to work correctly (no regression).

bug_fix_verification.bug_description: Admin-created users couldn't authorize transfers - verify-password endpoint returned 'Verification failed'
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: ObjectId(user_id) succeeds for 24-char hex STRING IDs but MongoDB lookup returns None because the _id is stored as STRING for admin-created users, not as ObjectId
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: All affected endpoints now try ObjectId first, then if result is None, try string lookup
tests_passed: test_02_admin_create_test_user - Admin can create test user with unique IBAN
test_08_regular_user_can_login - No regression for regular users
test_99_cleanup_test_user - Test user cleaned up successfully

frontend_verification.dashboard_display: Shows balance, accounts, recent activity, transfers
frontend_verification.send_money_flow: Transfer form opens correctly with all fields
frontend_verification.design: No issues noted - UI is clean and functional

seed_data_creation: Test user created and deleted during test run - no persistent test data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bug fix verified in iteration 160. Admin-created users (STRING IDs) can now verify passwords. The fix pattern: try ObjectId(user_id) first, then if result is None, try string lookup. All endpoints handling user_id have been fixed: /api/v1/auth/verify-password, transfers service, tickets router. Test file: test_admin_created_user_verify_password.py creates a test user via admin endpoint, tests all functionality, then cleans up by deleting the test user.
deployment_readiness: READY FOR DEPLOYMENT - Bug fix verified, no regressions
